prompt: A rectangular community swimming pool has a length of 25 meters, a width of 10 meters, and a uniform depth of 2.5 meters. The interior surfaces of the pool (the bottom and all four vertical sides) need to be resurfaced. The resurfacing material costs $15.00 per square meter. After resurfacing, the pool will be filled with water. Water costs $2.50 per cubic meter, but the community receives a special 20% discount on the water cost for the *first* 100 cubic meters of water. Any water volume beyond the initial 100 cubic meters is charged at the full price. Calculate the total combined cost for both resurfacing the pool and filling it completely.
